stem: A energy power grid uses 3 parallel generators for redundancy. The power maintained requires at least one generator to function. generator 1 has a reliability between 60% and 72% (uncertain due to limited operational data). generator 2 has a reliability of 82%. generator 3 has a reliability of 83%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all generators simultaneously, estimated between 1.7% and 6.0%. What is the overall probability that the power maintained?

ground_truth.answer: P(power maintained) ranges from 0.9285 to 0.9745. Point estimate: 0.952.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 generators fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.4 × 0.18 × 0.17 = 0.0122. P(works) = 0.9878.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.28 × 0.18 × 0.17 = 0.0086. P(works) = 0.9914.
With common-cause failure (1.7%-6.0%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9285, 0.9745].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
